posts.text:
Two photos;
One shows @BarbaraSlavin1’s smiling interview.
Two: thousands of victims and the man in the first photo ordered the massacre. 

That’s the reality behind the smiles.

 And now analysts, like Barbara, talk to media about the same killer Ali Larijani as if they are describing the leader of a democratic country.

She proudly says she interviewed him four times and even praises him as someone who supposedly wanted to resolve tensions with the United States.

That is simply not true.

Ali

در گفتگو با تلویزیون اسکای نیوز از انگلستان، از عملکرد سردبیر این رسانه انتقاد کردم و‌ گفتم درست در زمانی که خیابان‌های ایران هنوز بوی خون می‌داد و‌ در بیمارستان‌ها زخمی‌ها را کشتند و به پرستارها تجاوز کردند، سردبیر این رسانه رفت به سفارتخانه رژیم تا در جشن سالگرد پیروزی انقلاب اسلامی  شرکت کند به همراه نمایندگانی از نخست وزیر و دولت انگلیس.
خودتان بشنوید و قضاوت کنید. مهم نیست دیگر به این رسانه دعوت نشوم، بیان حقیقت مهمتر از راضی نگه داشتن این رسانه‌ها یا سیاستمداران است…


Masih Alinejad 🏳️ (@

A Sky News editor and representatives of prime Minister of UK @Keir_Starmer’s government attended a party at the Iran’s regime embassy in London celebrating the anniversary of the Islamic Republic. On the same days the regime’s security forces stormed a hospital and gang-raped nurses for helping wounded protesters.
Millions of victims’ families were heartbroken. So I raised it directly on Sky News.

They didn’t shared the interview on their social media so I asked them to send me the clip. Forgi

Ali Larijani, Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council of the Islamic Republic, was eliminated this morning.

Let’s remember who Ali Larijani was. He wasn’t some harmless bureaucrat. He was a national security boss key architect of the brutal crackdown on Iran’s recent protests which resulted in the massacre of more than 32,000 unarmed Iranians. When someone responsible for that level of brutality is removed, it is not a tragedy, it is a sign of justice for defenseless victims. 
In thi

This video went viral across the world, including in Germany.

Annalena Baerbock, is now serving at the United Nations General Assembly and this is her picture standing next to the Islamic Republic foreign minister.
Let me be clear: Germany has shown meaningful support for Iranian civil society and for victims of the regime. The Mayor of Berlin, the Berlin Parliament, and the German Parliament have all supported our work and the mission of the to promote freedom and democracy. I am grateful for

“If regime kills me in Iran, my family must pay money to get my body back and be forced to do a false confession on TV. But if I die in a U.S. strike, at least no one will force my family to pay money or lie.”

This is the frustration Iranians live with.

Video
  



Video

امروز در گفت‌وگو با اسکای‌نیوز به گزارش‌هایی اعتراض کردم که نشان می‌دهد مقام‌هایی از  وزارت خارجه بریتانیا و همچنین برخی خبرنگاران از جمله سردبیر امور بین‌الملل خود اسکای‌نیوز در مراسمی در سفارت جمهوری اسلامی ایران در لندن شرکت کردند برای جشن گرفتن سالگرد حکومتی که دستش به خون هزاران ایرانی آلوده است.

گفتم سردبیر اسکای‌نیوز در پاسخ به انتقاد ایرانیان گفته که خبرنگاران هر سال در چنین مراسمی شرکت می‌کنند و این بخشی از کار عادی روزنامه‌نگاری است.
اما واقعیت این است که جمهوری اسلامی یک حکومت عادی ن

Live on Sky News today, I raised concerns about reports that officials from @Keir_Starmer government, members of the UK Foreign Office, and journalists including the international affairs editor of Sky News, attended a celebration at the Iranian Embassy in London marking the anniversary of the Islamic Revolution.
I said that the editor of Sky News responded to criticism from Iranians by saying that journalists attend this embassy event every year as part of normal journalistic work. But let me e

After we secretly brought @kosareftekharii a young Iranian woman blinded in one eye by an Iran’s regime bullet during the Women, Life, Freedom revolution to Germany, I introduced her to two Western leaders: Annalena Baerbock, the feminist foreign minister of Germany, and Mark Rutte, who was then the Prime Minister of the Netherlands.
Watch their reactions. Watch their body language. Judge for yourself.

One bent down in respect and shook the hand of a victim of a terrorist regime. The other woul

Thank you @margbrennan for calling out the hypocrisy of @araghchi.
He claims he is “the voice of the Iranian people.” No. He is the voice of the regime that silences them. He’s not even a diplomat. He’s ISIS in a suit, speaking English to normalize a murder machine.
This is the same man who proudly went on television and bragged that the regime had killed more than 3,000 protesters. Today we know the number is over 32,000.

And the internet in Iran isn’t shut down for “security.” It’s shut down

I invite you anti-war activists in the West and I also invite the international media to speak with these Iranian women the regime shot in the eyes for protesting.

Look into our eyes. We carry the wounds of a war the Islamic Republic imposed on us.

We have a message for young people in America and Europe.

Let us talk about the real threat to democracy.

#LetUsTalk 👁️✌️

Video
  



Video
  




Masih Alinejad 🏳️ (@AlinejadMasih)

Mayor Mamdani, once you said you were ready to arrest those who

Iranian Women’s Soccer Players Withdraw Asylum Request !

Would anyone flee a dictatorship… then suddenly go back? I know their hostage diplomacy playbook very well.

The Iranian media now announced that Mona Hamoudi, Zahra Sarbali, and Zahra Meshkinkar, two players and a staff member of Iran’s women’s national football team, have withdrawn their asylum request in Australia and are heading back to Iran.

Right. Because women usually flee a dictatorship, they remove their for ed hijab and then su

Mayor Mamdani, once you said you were ready to arrest those who commit terror.

Well, here is the reality. Right now we have individuals openly praising terrorist organizations such as IRGC, Hamas, and Hezbollah, groups recognized as terrorists by the United States, Europe, and many democratic countries.

The IRGC hired assassins to kill me in New York. Three of them have already been convicted in a U.S. federal court.

I do not feel safe when these groups are glorified. It can embolden them to
Calling out Islamic Republic outside Federal Court in lower Manhattan
Ayatollah Khamenei is hiding alone like a rat: Masih Alinejad
Defining Your Mission: Love for Country or Anger at the Government
The Pain of 57 Mothers
Thanking Jack Dorsey: The Role of Twitter in Fighting Misinformation
The Last Time I Saw My Son at Liberty
The Arrest That Transformed a Mother-in-Law/Daughter-in-Law Relationship
The Fight for Freedom and the Bond of Family
Women Who Turned Pain into a Lifeline for Political Prisoners
Supporting freedom fighters is not an act of kindness.
